case: Switzerland d01 reinit-h36 REAL production column view, native (ny,nx,nz)=(128,128,44)=16384 cols
method: tiled (lax.scan over flattened-column tiles) vs untiled monolithic body; per-leaf BYTE equality on 16 state leaves + 4 precip channels (20 leaves)

root_cause_decomposition.reshape_only_flatten_restore_no_scan: bitwise_fail=0/20 (BYTE-EXACT) — the (ny,nx,nz)->(ncol,nz) flatten + restore is a pure bijection, introduces NO bit change
root_cause_decomposition.multi_tile_lax_scan: bitwise_fail=4/20, worst 2.84e-14 K on T — IDENTICAL diff for tile=4096/5000/8192, so it is the scan-vs-monolith XLA fusion-boundary FMA contraction, NOT a pad/logic bug
root_cause_decomposition.conclusion: Residual is the documented Tier-P fusion-boundary phenomenon (V0150-TIERED-IDENTITY-ADR): op-identical math, last-bit differs when fusion boundaries move. Column tiling moves them by construction.

tier_p_assessment.verdict: TIER_P_PASS — sub-ulp, ~1.9e-14 of the frozen T limit; cannot move any operational gate

ship_decision.rationale: Production 128x128=16384 cols == one tile so tiling NEVER engages in the default forecast anyway; defaulting OFF keeps the v0.15 default graph BYTE-IDENTICAL to v0.14 (passes the strict gate for what actually ships). Tiling is an OPT-IN large-grid (>16384-col) VRAM lever with documented Tier-P sub-ulp deviation.

source_fix: src/gpuwrf/physics/thompson_column.py _maybe_tiled_thompson_full: activation gate was ndim==2 but the production column view is (ny,nx,nz) 3-D, so tiling NEVER engaged in production (silently inert on every forecast, never a passing GPU proof). Fix flattens leading horizontal axes to a single column axis (byte-exact reshape) so tiling can actually engage on the real layout. New 3-D regression tests in tests/test_v015_mp_column_tiling.py.
